rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MELVIN FLEMING
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/26/23. (No earlier availability.) 1. A second amended petition was just filed on 6/15/22 (but not e-filed until 6/22/23). No courtesy copy has been received yet by the examiner. 2. An amended petition requires the same notice as the petition it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] 3. Notice may not be mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] The courtesy copy of the second amended petition and proof of service of notice of the second amended petition hearing on 9/26/23 must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/15/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and REPRESENTATIVE CAPACITY, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DHIREN DWIJEN DESAI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/26/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of the Notice of Hearing has been filed. Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (2) Creditors, whose allowed claims have not yet been paid, must also receive notice. Allowed claims must be paid before an estate can close. (Allowed but unpaid claims ARE liabilities of the estate.) (3) The Corrected I&A reflects non-cash assets that a personal representative may not appraise. [Probate Code Sections 8901 and 8902.] (The boxes in item 4 of an I&A should NOT be checked when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed.) (4) The assets on hand as of the date of death, as shown in the Corrected I&A, totaled $93,102.08, but the accounting begins with $102,394.54, and Schedule A differs from her Corrected I&A assets. (5) The accounting period exceeds the number of years this Court permits for an initial accounting. [LRSF 14.34 A.] (6) Schedule B of the accounting is not detailed. Lump sums are not sufficient; each receipt must be set forth. [Probate Code Section 1062(a).] (7) Original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (8) Notice must also be given to the Director of Health Care Services, if the decedent was the surviving spouse of a person who received Medi-Cal benefits. AFTER the Probate Referee has appraised the non-cash assets as of the date of death and a Second Corrected I&A is filed, the petition must be amended. Notice must be given of the amended petition after it is e-filed. [CRC 7.53(a).] If the Probate Referee cannot complete the appraisal in time for the accounting and petition to be amended and notice of amended petition to be given for the hearing on 9/26/23, Counsel should contact the examiner to request more time. Alternatively, the heirs could waive the accounting. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/15/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] All pleadings must show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SONIA GUTIERREZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/26/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Still no courtesy copy of the petition has been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) A court-appointed fiduciary must sign and verify her own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment thereto, not her attorney. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/15/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOUGLAS S. CHURCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/5/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An attorney's "declaration" is not appropriate when a person seeks appointment as a fiduciary. [CRC 7.103(c).] The PETITIONER'S verified SUPPLEMENT to her second amended petition must be filed to supplement it with a complete copy of the will, to set forth the address of the decedent's son, and to address, or attach the will drafting attorney's explanation of, the discrepancy in the dates of execution and attestation of the first codicil. (2) The only correct notice at the opening of an estate is the mandatory Judicial Council form NOTICE OF [SECOND AMENDED] PETITION TO ADMINISTER ESTATE. A courtesy copy of (1) the PETITIONER'S supplement to her second amended petition and (2) the proof of service of the Notice of Second Amended Petition to Administer Estate, showing the 9/5/23 hearing date must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 8/28/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Pleadings should stand on their own and not "respond" to issues posted in a tentative ruling.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM E. SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The estate cash on hand exceeding $540,000 is not fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit. The executor has not taken any action to transfer the uninsured portion of the estate cash to insured accounts at different institutions (see Probate Code sections 46, 9700, & 10533(a)) or to purchase investment(s) specifically authorized by section 9730. It should be noted that counsel for the executor argued the same issue in the matter of the Estate of Stephen Kopel (PES-20-303619) at the hearing on the final distribution on 06/30/2021. See the minutes of that hearing ("The Court noted that this matter is on for hearing for Mr. McNitt to argue his position regarding the requirement to deposit estate funds into federally insured financial institutions. Mr. McNitt stated that he wants to be educated on this issue, that he does not see it as mandatory, referred to Probate Code 10533(a) and stated that he does not think there is a mandate. The Court asked where's the authority to put money in uninsured accounts. Mr. McNitt stated that there is none. The Court stated that this is the limitation that is set by the Legislature, referred to section 46 of the probate code, stated that it will not allow funds to be placed in financial institution that are not federally insured and that having uninsured accounts is an incredible risk. The Court also stated that in the future if Mr. McNitt counsels his clients not to open federally insured accounts, it will require full bond, spoke of and named multiple credit unions that have been liquidated, stated that it does not know why anyone would counsel their Clients to take that risk, and spoke of sweep accounts that will move funds to ensure that they are all insured.") PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LATONYA GRACE ROGERS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Existence And Internal Affairs Of A Trust And To Determine Whether Successor Trustee John David Barry Shall Be Deemed To Have Predeceased Settlor Latonya Rogers By Clear And Convincing Evidence Of Undue Influence
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/01/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 06/06/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) No proposed order (hard copy) has been received. 3) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(1) and actually served with a copy of the DE-120 form must be attached to the proof of service. 4) If the petitioner is not a current or former beneficiary or trustee of the case name trust, does the petitioner have standing to seek relief under Probate Code section 17200(a) concerning the existence or internal affairs of that trust? 5) If no conservator has been appointed for the settlor of the case name trust, does the probate department have concurrent jurisdiction over the financial abuse claim under section 15657.3 of the Welfare & Institutions Code? Even if the probate department is to accept the jurisdiction, no summons has been issued and served on John Barry. 6) If the petitioner is requesting an order directing John Barry to transfer the subject real property (or proceeds if sold) to the trustee of Eddie Rogers' trust (or to the personal representative of the estate of Latonya Rogers), should the petitioner be required to amend the petition to seek the relief under Probate Code sections 850 & 856?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRED LAZARUS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Rodney Roberts and Theresa Roberts, the decedent's maternal cousins entitled to notice of this petition and partial distribution from this estate, cannot be located despite the petitioner's efforts described in her declaration as supplemented. 2) The petitioner is a nominee of Stephen Wilson, one of the decedent's 7 cousins/heirs. The other 6 cousins (including 2 cousins who cannot be located) have higher priority than the petitioner as other persons of the class of the person making the nomination under Probate Code section 8465(c), but have not waived their priority. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEAN MILDRED NELSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/26/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No will has been lodged yet. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) If the whereabouts of an interested party are unknown, an ex parte petition must be filed to dispense with notice to such party. [CRC 7.52.] (5) If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Sec. 8113.] (6) Is Sandra E. Olson the same personal as Susan Elna Olson Ingebritson? (7) The heir chart does not indicate whether paternal heirs Hans R. Christensson, Ingrid Elise Carlsson, and Paul F. Kallin and maternal heir Leslie K. Olson were survived by issue. (8) No courtesy copies of papers filed after 6/8/23 have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/15/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (This Court no longer requires the local form, Declaration of Real Property.)
